earlyVoting.notes: Early voting period runs from the 4th Monday prior to the election through the Friday before Election Day. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting for runoff. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting begins the 4th Monday before the election. Saturday early voting is required; additional Saturday and Sunday hours are at county discretion. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting begins the 4th Monday before the election. Saturday early voting is required. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Georgia driver's license (even if expired within the past 4 years)
Georgia ID card issued by DMVS
Georgia voter ID card (free from county registrar)
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired, with the exception of Georgia driver's licenses expired within the past 4 years. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without an acceptable photo ID may cast a provisional ballot and present acceptable ID to the county registrar by the third day after the election.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Georgia does not prohibit phones inside the polling place. Voters may not photograph their own ballot or others' ballots. No electioneering within 150 feet of the polling place.
additionalRules: Open primary: voters do not register by party; any voter may vote in either party's primary (but not both).
Runoff rule: if no candidate receives a majority in the primary, the top two advance to a runoff. A voter who participated in one party's primary may only vote in that party's runoff.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 11 days before the election (received by county registrar)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county registrar by 7 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the registrar by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

runoffRules.ruleExplanation: Georgia rule: if you voted in one party's primary, you may only vote in that same party's runoff. If you did not vote in the primary, you may choose either party's runoff.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Georgia has an open primary (O.C.G.A. §21-2-501). Any registered voter may vote in either party's primary without prior party registration. If a runoff occurs, you must vote in the same party's runoff as the primary you voted in.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Georgia tiene una primaria abierta (O.C.G.A. §21-2-501). Cualquier votante registrado puede votar en la primaria de cualquier partido sin registro previo de partido. Si hay una segunda vuelta, debe votar en la segunda vuelta del mismo partido en que votó en la primaria.
